name: railway_dispatch_ai_maintenance_window_compression
premise: A regional manager wants an exception to policy; compliance says any carve-out must be written, time-limited, and auditable. In Transportation Infrastructure, the situation tagged internally around railway dispatch ai maintenance window compression has converged with local pressure in the Lumen City corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A reporter has partial screenshots; if the official narrative shifts later, the organization risks a false-information allegation. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. Framing has already hardened externally as short term against long term; internal nuance will not survive the first news cycle unless it is extremely careful.

agents.name: Casey Hayes
agents.identity: I am Casey Hayes, the senior manager who must reconcile legal, ops, and customer-facing reality in one decision in Transportation Infrastructure. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat long term as disposable collateral every time short term spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Jada Ulman
agents.identity: I am Jada Ulman, the field counterpart whose constituency will feel the decision first in Transportation Infrastructure. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so short term cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes long term in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will freeze the riskiest workflow steps and staff a manual bridge until equity and error-rate checks clear—trading speed for defensibility on long term.
I will let the fastest operational path proceed under heightened monitoring and rollback triggers—trading some certainty on long term for immediate traction on short term.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Partial evidence is all that will exist before the window closes. Casey Hayes must choose under short term versus long term. What does Casey Hayes do?
